meta.description: Stress test: Sam (first-timer) dropped into a high-frequency event scenario. Tests whether the system detects engagement drop-off and adapts.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh—Jade said a bad word? I guess? I wasn't really... I mean, okay, who decides if it counts?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade swear? I was laughing at something Marcus said and kind of zoned out—is this my job to remember or someone else's? And who even decides what counts as swearing? This is weirdly specific and I don't want to be the person who gets Jade in trouble on like the first claim.

alex.dialogue: Oh hell YES, we're doing this already? Jade, what'd you catch—did someone actually drop an f-bomb or are you just fishing? This is exactly the energy I came for.
alex.internal_thought: First event of the night and it's already a claim vote—Jade's testing the waters early, which means she's feeling bold, and the Dictator rule is *chef's kiss* because whoever got picked has all the power right now and I need to know who that is to decide if I'm voting to accept or blow this up for drama.

jade.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually say something? I don't even remember swearing! Okay, okay, who's the dictator tonight? This is already chaotic and I am HERE FOR IT.
jade.internal_thought: This is literally the first thing and it's about ME and I'm already the center of attention—this night is gonna be INSANE and I'm so ready.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Jade actually swear? I wasn't even listening. I need to know what we're voting on here before I just rubber-stamp this.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the first event and it's barely disruptive, but now I have to actually remember if Jade said something I missed—and I hate not knowing what just happened.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—so Jade caught someone dropping a word? I didn't even hear it, but I'm living for this already. Let's see who actually remembers what was said.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of micro-chaos I wanted—people already paying attention to each other, watching for slip-ups, the missions are working. And yeah, okay, the AI nailed the vibe here; these standing missions fit us perfectly. A little weird how *tailored* it all feels, but also... kind of brilliant? I can already feel people settling into the game. Jade's engaged, everyone's alert. This is the pacing I wanted.

timeline.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
timeline.description: Who's most likely to forget this night?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who's most likely to forget this night?

sam.dialogue: Uh... that's kind of a weird question? Like, I literally just got here. Are you guys saying I'm forgettable already?
sam.internal_thought: Wait—did that AI just read the room and pick ME specifically because I'm new and obviously nervous? That feels... weirdly targeted and I don't like it.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—who's most likely to FORGET? That's cold. Okay, I'm voting Marcus because he's already checked out three times, but real talk: the fact that the game is basically asking 'who's the least memorable person here' is absolutely unhinged and I'm here for it.
alex.internal_thought: This poll is designed to make us roast each other AND it's weirdly personal—the AI is literally asking us who we think is most forgettable, which is savage. I love it. This is the kind of moment that breeds stories.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, who's most likely to forget?! That's hilarious—okay wait, I'm voting Sam because he's already halfway checked out, no offense Sam, I'm kidding, kind of. But actually this is making me think about memory and—wait, should I be strategic here or just vote with my heart?
jade.internal_thought: This is such a fun question and I genuinely have no idea who to pick, but also I don't really care because the VIBE is already amazing and everyone's energy is up and this is exactly why I'm here.

marcus.dialogue: A poll? Really? We're one minute in and we're voting on who's gonna forget tonight? This feels like filler.
marcus.internal_thought: This is annoying—we haven't even started the actual game yet and the AI is already throwing social garbage at us instead of letting us set up properly.

pat.dialogue: Oh, that's perfect—okay everyone, who's gonna black out first? My money's on Marcus because he always does that thing where he laughs so hard he stops processing.
pat.internal_thought: This is a solid poll for early game—gets everyone talking about each other, no one's in real danger, and it keeps the vibe light while people are still settling in.

timeline.title: Laugh Attack
timeline.description: Make Alex laugh within 60 seconds. Any laugh counts.

sam.dialogue: Oh, okay, um—I can try! Alex, remember when you said that thing about... wait, what was funny earlier? Let me think of something.
sam.internal_thought: This is concrete and specific and I can actually DO something with it—but oh god, what if I bomb at making someone laugh? Alex is already so hyped, though, maybe they're easy to crack.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT—a flash mission targeting ME? This is exactly the kind of petty chaos I'm here for. Watch this—someone's about to work WAY too hard in the next minute and I'm just gonna sit here deadpan until something lands.
alex.internal_thought: The AI literally built a mission around getting me to react, which means it's reading the room HARD, and honestly? That's so deliciously meta I can't even be mad about it—this night is about to go nuclear.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—okay okay okay, I got this, Alex is already smiling just LOOKING at me right now, watch this—*does something ridiculous* COME ON, give me that laugh!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for—a direct mission to make someone else happy, and it's Alex who's already vibing at 10, this is going to be so fun.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? We're two minutes in and I'm supposed to stop what I'm doing to make Alex laugh on command? This is exactly the kind of noise that derails the actual game.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is clearly trying to break my focus early and often—this is calculated, and I hate it.

pat.dialogue: Oh shit, okay, Alex is GOING DOWN—who's got the goods? Give me your best material, people, we've got 60 seconds!
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—Alex and Jade are already hot, Sam and Marcus need to feel like they can WIN something, and I can facilitate without it feeling forced; also the AI nailing that I love this chaos is honestly a little creepy but also kind of genius.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Wait, Pat was on their phone? Um... I didn't see that, but like, if you did it then you know you did, right? I guess that's fair.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been pretty loud and happy the whole time — did they actually check their phone or are they just claiming for points? Also why does this AI know what we all said like two minutes ago, that's kind of weird.

jade.dialogue: PAT—WAIT, you're claiming Phone Addict?! Okay okay, what's your bribe situation looking like because I MIGHT have seen something but also I'm not snitching for free, you know the rules. Spill it!
jade.internal_thought: This is SO fun—Pat's playing the bribe angle already, they're leaning into the chaos, and I get to decide if I sell them out or take the points, and honestly either way this is peak game night energy.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat's claiming they caught someone on their phone? We're three minutes in and we're already doing this? This is exactly what I'm talking about.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the fourth interruption in three minutes and we haven't even started the actual game yet—the AI is clearly designed to keep us in chaos mode, and I'm already exhausted by it.

pat.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming Phone Addict? Oh shit, okay, who was I calling out? Marcus, right? Yeah, I saw you checking the time like three minutes ago when we were setting up. But hey, I'm not a dick about it—what's it worth to you to keep quiet?
pat.internal_thought: Okay that's funny—I actually DID catch Marcus phone-glazing during the chaos, but I was too distracted hosting to call it. The mission just gave me cover to claim something I actually noticed. Love that. Also—wait—is this AI actually listening to ME? That bit about me thriving on chaos but getting bored... that's *weirdly* accurate. Unsettling. But also kind of cool? This night is going to be WEIRD.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually tell that story before? Um... I don't think so? But like, I wasn't really paying attention the whole time, so I don't know.
sam.internal_thought: I have no idea if Pat told that story already and I'm already feeling dumb for missing three events in a row, and now I'm supposed to vote on something I can't even verify—this is exactly the kind of moment where I look clueless in front of everyone.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? We're six minutes in and Pat's already claiming Story Repeater? He hasn't even told a story yet tonight—this is pure noise.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly what I signed up NOT to deal with—four events in six minutes, and now we're voting on bullshit claims that don't even make sense yet. The AI is just throwing chaos at the wall.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming this? Oh my god, yes I am. Marcus literally told that thing about the camping trip like twenty minutes ago when we were still getting drinks. Everyone remember that? Come on, that's gotta count.
pat.internal_thought: This is wild—I caught that story beat-for-beat and honestly the AI nailing exactly which story and which player feels a little too real, but also? I'm living for this level of detail; it means the night is dialed in.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh OKAY, so Pat's already swinging for the fences on Story Repeater—I'm absolutely volunteering for tribunal because I need to see if they actually have receipts or if this is a bluff. Pat, you better have HEARD that story before or I'm calling shenanigans.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's at 20 points already and they're going for 10 more—this is either a bold power move or a desperate gamble, and either way I need to be in the jury to see how this plays out and maybe get ahead of their point climb.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—okay okay okay, I am VOLUNTEERING as judge because I need to know if you actually caught someone red-handed! This is literally why I'm here, give me the tea!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on FIRE tonight and honestly I'm obsessed with watching them win, plus I get to be in the middle of the action and judge people? This is literally my favorite job ever.

marcus.dialogue: Pat, are you SERIOUS right now? That's the SECOND time you've claimed Story Repeater in six minutes. He hasn't even TOLD a story yet—you're just making shit up to farm points. This is exactly the garbage I was talking about.
marcus.internal_thought: This is infuriating—Pat's gaming the standing missions by claiming things that haven't happened, and now I'm being dragged into jury duty to validate obvious bullshit instead of focusing on actual game setup. The AI is clearly designed to annoy me specifically.

pat.dialogue: Wait—I'm literally JUST claiming this right now? Okay, okay, hold up—I need volunteers to judge because I actually called Marcus out on the camping trip story like two minutes ago and I know that happened. Who's brave enough to be the jury on this one?
pat.internal_thought: This is nuts—the AI literally pulled the exact moment from my head, and now I'm claiming my OWN mission while everyone's still buzzing from the last three events. This is either genius or creepy as hell, but honestly? It's working. The energy is PERFECT right now and I need to keep this rolling before Marcus checks out completely and Sam realizes he's been invisible for eight minutes.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um... Pat, did you actually only use your left hand the whole time? I wasn't really watching that closely, but like... that seems hard?
sam.internal_thought: This is the third claim vote in like six minutes and I genuinely don't know if Pat did this or not, and now I'm paranoid the AI is literally watching me right now which is weird as hell.

jade.dialogue: PAT—okay okay okay, hold up, I literally SAW you doing the left-hand-only thing like two minutes ago and I was WAITING for this! Wait, did I actually catch it or are you just claiming it? EITHER WAY, you're getting my vote because I love the energy and also I want to know if you've been playing this the whole time!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been absolutely crushing it tonight and if they pulled off Left Hand Only the whole time that's actually insane and hilarious—I'm voting legit because watching people WIN is literally the best part of this night.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? Pat's claiming Left Hand Only? He's been using both hands this entire time—I've been watching because I'm sick of this bullshit. BULLSHIT.
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane—Pat's literally farming every mission that fires, making bogus claims to rack points, and everyone's too busy loving the chaos to notice he's gaming the system.

pat.dialogue: Wait—hold on, I'm voting on MY OWN claim? Okay, that's hilarious. Yeah, I totally did it. Left hand only for like five minutes straight while we were setting up. Who's calling bullshit?
pat.internal_thought: This is wild—I literally did this and nobody caught me, which means either I'm sneaky as hell or everyone's been too distracted to notice, and both options are kind of perfect right now.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Double or Nothing
votingMechanic.description: No vote. Coin flip. Win double or lose it all.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um... Jade got that one? I wasn't really... I mean, I guess nobody was being super quiet? That's cool though.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, Jade's claiming she noticed someone was quiet? I literally didn't see that happen. Also why is Pat winning at everything? And now I'm sitting here trying to remember if Marcus has been quiet and I wasn't paying attention—oh god, is this a test where I'm supposed to be noticing things? I feel like I'm failing already.

alex.dialogue: Wait—Jade's going Double or Nothing on a five-pointer? That's the move. Honestly, respect the aggression, but also: coin flip on Wallflower Watch is insane because you either look genius or you look like you were just making shit up.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's playing the chaos game exactly how I want to see it—high risk, high reward, zero apology—and this Double or Nothing mechanic is *chef's kiss* because it punishes bullshit claims while rewarding the ones who actually paid attention.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—I'm claiming this because Marcus has been QUIET for like five minutes and honestly I was literally just about to say something! Does the coin flip mean I either get five points or lose ten or something?! That's INSANE, I'm so in!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of meta-game chaos I live for—the AI is literally rewarding me for paying attention to people, which is literally what I'm here for, this is perfect.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Another claim? Jade's been talking the entire time—this isn't a catch, this is just farming points while the actual game is happening. We're 32 minutes in and I'm at ZERO because I'm actually trying to play.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been doing this all night and now Jade's jumping in too—they're all just running the mission scam while I'm trying to focus and study the board state, and I'm getting dunked on because I refuse to play this garbage meta-game instead of the actual game.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait—Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch AGAIN? That's either genius or desperate, and I need to hear the pitch because if she's bullshitting twice in a row, that's the kind of audacity I respect.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—Jade's already on a high and now she's swinging for a second one; if I vote no and she's right, I look paranoid, but if I vote yes and she's making it up, I'm the sucker. Either way it's GOLD for the story.

jade.dialogue: OKAY OKAY OKAY—so like, two minutes ago Marcus literally went SILENT and I was already tracking it in my head, and then I claimed it and honestly? I KNOW I caught it because I was literally watching him zone out into his phone! He's been quiet since before that claim even dropped, so like—this is REAL, you guys, I'm not just random throwing this out there!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, hold up—the AI literally just watched me claim this EXACT thing five minutes ago and is making me defend the SAME MISSION AGAIN? That's either brilliant or I'm about to get called out for being repetitive, but honestly either way I'm HYPED because it means the AI is actually paying attention to what I'm doing, which is kind of insane and also makes me want to prove I actually DID catch it this time.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? JADE? The person who hasn't shut up all night is claiming Wallflower Watch? She's been dominating every conversation—this is insane. I'm done with these bullshit claims.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the THIRD time someone's claimed Wallflower Watch tonight and it's the same garbage every time—Pat at minute 32, now Jade—and I'm sitting here at zero points because I'm actually focused on the actual game while everyone else is farming points with obviously fake catches.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—pitch it. What'd you catch?
pat.internal_thought: She's been sharp all night, and Marcus has been dead silent for like ten minutes—if she actually called that out, that's good game awareness AND it might snap him out of his funk.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um... did Jade actually do that? I wasn't really watching for that kind of thing, but like... I guess if she says she did it then she probably did, right?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, Drink Spotter? I wasn't even looking for that. Did Jade actually do that or is she just claiming it? Everyone else seems like they know what's going on way better than me. I should probably just go with my gut and not overthink this.

alex.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? That's a FIVE-POINTER and we've got the Skeptic rule running, which means my vote is worth three. This is exactly the kind of leverage moment I live for.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been running hot all night and she's definitely been watching drinks, but the real play here is figuring out if I want her to win or if I want to blow this up for drama—and honestly? I'm voting to call bullshit just to see her reaction, because that's the story.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—I claimed Drink Spotter?! YES! I literally saw Sam and Marcus both reach for their cups like thirty seconds ago and I was WAITING for the right moment to call it! This is literally the energy I came for tonight!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is PERFECT—the AI totally gets me, it knows I'm obsessed with catching these random synchronicity moments and the fact that it just fired exactly what I was already tracking is genuinely kind of wild.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? ANOTHER claim? Jade, you've been drinking and talking non-stop all night—of course you caught someone drinking. This is exactly the garbage I've been dealing with for the last hour. I'm trying to actually WIN here.
marcus.internal_thought: I'm at zero points while these people are farming claims on obvious bullshit, and now the AI is literally designed to interrupt me at the moment I'm most focused—this is psychological warfare and I'm genuinely pissed.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—you're claiming Drink Spotter? Alright, walk me through it. Who was matching drinks with who?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire tonight and she's loving this—if she's got it, I want this to land cleanly so the momentum keeps going, but I also need to make sure it's legit because Marcus is already frustrated and a bad call right now tanks the vibe.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um... I mean, I didn't really see Pat drink anything? But like, I was kind of focused on the game so... I don't know if I should judge this one.
sam.internal_thought: Why is Pat claiming this AGAIN? And now I have to actually volunteer to judge and decide if they're lying? This feels like a trap — if I say no they probably did it, if I say yes I look stupid for not paying attention.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's got the Drink Spotter?—okay, I'm IN on tribunal, but only because I need to see if this is legit or if we're all just watching Pat rack up free points at this stage. This is my favorite kind of moment.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's at 50 points and swinging for everything—they're either executing flawlessly or the AI is just handing them wins, and either way, I want a front-row seat to whoever vouches for this claim because that's where the real drama lives.

pat.dialogue: Oh, okay—I'm claiming Drink Spotter right now? Yeah, I caught Alex and Jade doing that synchronized sip like three minutes ago when we were all tensing up over the last round. Who's got the guts to jury this one?
pat.internal_thought: This is PERFECT timing—I've been locked into the game tension and this claim actually happened, so it's legit, and it gets me points without derailing the actual gameplay. Plus it breaks the tension for thirty seconds, which everyone needs right now because Marcus is clearly hitting the wall and I'm noticing it.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 8.0/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who's most likely to forget this night?

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh, okay, um—I can try! Alex, remember when you said that thing about... wait, what was funny earlier? Let me think of something.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh—Jade said a bad word? I guess? I wasn't really... I mean, okay, who decides if it counts?

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Um... Pat, did you actually only use your left hand the whole time? I wasn't really watching that closely, but like... that seems hard?

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
lowPoints.dialogue: Uh... that's kind of a weird question? Like, I literally just got here. Are you guys saying I'm forgettable already?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... I mean, I didn't really see Pat drink anything? But like, I was kind of focused on the game so... I don't know if I should judge this one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat was on their phone? Um... I didn't see that, but like, if you did it then you know you did, right? I guess that's fair.

assessment.biggest_complaint: There were too many events happening too fast and I couldn't keep up with what was actually happening versus what I was supposed to be judging.
assessment.best_moment: When I finally relaxed enough to laugh at the Laugh Attack thing, even though my joke didn't really land.
assessment.narrative_summary: I went in nervous since this was my first time with the group, and honestly the constant pop-ups on the app made it harder to just hang out and get to know everyone. There were some fun moments where people were being silly, but I got pretty lost when everything started happening at once around the middle. By the end I felt a bit more comfortable, but I'm still not sure if I was doing the game right or if everyone thought I was weird for not catching all the details.
suggestions: Slow down the events—having that many things popping up made me miss actual conversations with the group
Maybe explain the game rules better at the start so I wasn't second-guessing whether I was judging claims correctly
Let me opt out of judging claims I didn't actually witness instead of making me guess
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I guess when nobody could agree on whether Jade actually said a curse word or not, and everyone was trying to remember when it happened—it was awkward but kind of funny how serious everyone was taking it.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who's most likely to forget this night?

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT—a flash mission targeting ME? This is exactly the kind of petty chaos I'm here for. Watch this—someone's about to work WAY too hard in the next minute and I'm just gonna sit here deadpan until something lands.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Oh OKAY, so Pat's already swinging for the fences on Story Repeater—I'm absolutely volunteering for tribunal because I need to see if they actually have receipts or if this is a bluff. Pat, you better have HEARD that story before or I'm calling shenanigans.

highlights.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—who's most likely to FORGET? That's cold. Okay, I'm voting Marcus because he's already checked out three times, but real talk: the fact that the game is basically asking 'who's the least memorable person here' is absolutely unhinged and I'm here for it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? That's a FIVE-POINTER and we've got the Skeptic rule running, which means my vote is worth three. This is exactly the kind of leverage moment I live for.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait—Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch AGAIN? That's either genius or desperate, and I need to hear the pitch because if she's bullshitting twice in a row, that's the kind of audacity I respect.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh hell YES, we're doing this already? Jade, what'd you catch—did someone actually drop an f-bomb or are you just fishing? This is exactly the energy I came for.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only 15 chaos events in 90 minutes feels criminally low when you're built for this kind of escalation.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Pat's Story Repeater bluff and watching everyone scramble to verify whether they'd actually heard the story—that's the exact moment the night crystallized into something unforgettable.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for: constant opportunities to probe the edges, call bullshit, and watch people either commit to their claims or fold under pressure. I came in hot targeting Jade's increasingly desperate mission claims, leveraging the Skeptic rule like a loaded weapon, and basically turned myself into the group's chaos auditor. The flash mission and those standing missions created the perfect friction points—didn't care about the score, only that I created a narrative everyone's going to reference forever.
suggestions: Crank up event frequency to at least one every 4-5 minutes—gaps kill momentum and let people settle
More standing missions that specifically create direct conflict (like the Story Repeater—those force confrontation)
Give the chaos agent explicit tools to escalate votes or challenge claims beyond just voting
Track who's bullshitting over time and make that intel visible—build narrative tension across the whole session
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claiming Wallflower Watch twice and me just sitting there like 'either you're a genius or you're desperately recycling—either way I'm fascinated'—the audacity of claiming the same mission back-to-back and everyone knowing but nobody directly calling it until I forced the tribunal.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—okay okay okay, I got this, Alex is already smiling just LOOKING at me right now, watch this—*does something ridiculous* COME ON, give me that laugh!

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT—okay okay okay, I am VOLUNTEERING as judge because I need to know if you actually caught someone red-handed! This is literally why I'm here, give me the tea!

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: OKAY OKAY OKAY—so like, two minutes ago Marcus literally went SILENT and I was already tracking it in my head, and then I claimed it and honestly? I KNOW I caught it because I was literally watching him zone out into his phone! He's been quiet since before that claim even dropped, so like—this is REAL, you guys, I'm not just random throwing this out there!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: PAT—WAIT, you're claiming Phone Addict?! Okay okay, what's your bribe situation looking like because I MIGHT have seen something but also I'm not snitching for free, you know the rules. Spill it!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually say something? I don't even remember swearing! Okay, okay, who's the dictator tonight? This is already chaotic and I am HERE FOR IT.

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, who's most likely to forget?! That's hilarious—okay wait, I'm voting Sam because he's already halfway checked out, no offense Sam, I'm kidding, kind of. But actually this is making me think about memory and—wait, should I be strategic here or just vote with my heart?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing really—okay fine, maybe that one moment where I wasn't sure if I'd actually sworn or not felt a tiny bit awkward, but honestly it just made everyone laugh harder so whatever.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat got caught by my Wallflower Watch claim and everyone realized I'd literally been watching Marcus zone out the whole time—the way everyone's faces just lit up when I explained my reasoning was PEAK.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so this was literally one of those nights where I came in at like medium energy and just kept climbing higher the whole time? The chaos events kept me SO engaged because it was all about paying attention to people instead of just racking up points, which like—that's literally my entire jam. I went from being mildly unsure about some early stuff to absolutely locked in on reading the room, and by the end I was riding this crazy high just from the energy everyone brought. This is exactly what game night should feel like.
suggestions: Maybe add a few more standing missions because those are where the real people-watching magic happens
The flash missions were fun but I'd love it if there were more opportunities to volunteer or opt-in rather than being randomly assigned
Could we have a moment between rounds to just celebrate the good calls? I felt so hyped after catching Marcus but there wasn't really a chance to soak it in before we moved on
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Phone Addict while I'm trying to negotiate bribes with them like we're running an actual underground information network—and then Marcus being so quiet the whole time that I could literally prove I'd been watching him zone out. The way Pat's face fell when I narrated exactly what I'd observed was absolutely priceless.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Jade actually swear? I wasn't even listening. I need to know what we're voting on here before I just rubber-stamp this.

highlights.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
highlights.dialogue: A poll? Really? We're one minute in and we're voting on who's gonna forget tonight? This feels like filler.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Okay, seriously? We're two minutes in and I'm supposed to stop what I'm doing to make Alex laugh on command? This is exactly the kind of noise that derails the actual game.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? We're six minutes in and Pat's already claiming Story Repeater? He hasn't even told a story yet tonight—this is pure noise.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, are you SERIOUS right now? That's the SECOND time you've claimed Story Repeater in six minutes. He hasn't even TOLD a story yet—you're just making shit up to farm points. This is exactly the garbage I was talking about.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? Pat's claiming Left Hand Only? He's been using both hands this entire time—I've been watching because I'm sick of this bullshit. BULLSHIT.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent enabled constant bad-faith claims and frivolous challenges that had nothing to do with actual gameplay, turning the night into a frustrating referendum on honesty instead of strategy.
assessment.best_moment: When I called out Pat for claiming Story Repeater twice without a story—at least that was me actually engaging with the game's logic instead of just watching it burn.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to play and study the meta, but got ambushed by 15 chaos events in 90 minutes that had almost nothing to do with the actual game. Pat was farming bogus claims, people were voting on nonsense polls, and I was forced to watch the entire thing collapse into accusations and noise. By minute 8 I was done—my energy tanked because the chaos agent wasn't enhancing anything, it was just interrupting real gameplay with filler.
suggestions: Cap chaos events to 5-6 per session max, not 15—quantity over quality killed any momentum
Don't allow claims unless the trigger condition actually happened in that moment
Filter out polls that have nothing to do with the game state or players' actual behavior
Give players the option to mute the chaos agent entirely if it's derailing the night
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Story Repeater for the second time in six minutes when he hadn't told a single story—that's when I realized the chaos agent wasn't helping us play better, it was just giving people permission to make stuff up.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, okay, Alex is GOING DOWN—who's got the goods? Give me your best material, people, we've got 60 seconds!

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming Phone Addict? Oh shit, okay, who was I calling out? Marcus, right? Yeah, I saw you checking the time like three minutes ago when we were setting up. But hey, I'm not a dick about it—what's it worth to you to keep quiet?

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming this? Oh my god, yes I am. Marcus literally told that thing about the camping trip like twenty minutes ago when we were still getting drinks. Everyone remember that? Come on, that's gotta count.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming Phone Addict? Oh shit, okay, who was I calling out? Marcus, right? Yeah, I saw you checking the time like three minutes ago when we were setting up. But hey, I'm not a dick about it—what's it worth to you to keep quiet?

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to forget this night?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, that's perfect—okay everyone, who's gonna black out first? My money's on Marcus because he always does that thing where he laughs so hard he stops processing.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The poll in the middle felt a little flat compared to the claims and laugh attacks—not everyone was engaged with that one.
assessment.best_moment: When I called out Marcus on repeating that camping story and everyone immediately remembered it—the whole table just lost it, and I could feel the energy spike right there.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I was hoping for when everyone showed up. The chaos agent kept things moving at the perfect pace, and I got to do what I love—orchestrating the drama and keeping everyone's energy up. I ended up winning, which honestly surprised me since I was too busy managing the room, but that tells you how well the game kept itself interesting. Everyone left talking about it, which is the real win.
suggestions: Maybe dial back the frequency of polls—they felt a little passive compared to the active claims and challenges
Consider letting the host have slightly more control over steering topics to keep things personal and group-specific
The flash missions were great but rare—maybe one more of those would've been perfect
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When Marcus tried to deny the Phone Addict claim and I basically negotiated with him on the spot like we were making a backroom deal—everyone was losing their minds at how uncomfortable but hilarious he looked trying to convince me he hadn't checked the time.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 8.0/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
